name: Doubao-Seed-1.6
note: Adaptive-CoT multimodal flagship, 256K context, ~63% cheaper than Doubao 1.5; spans standard/thinking/flash variants.

results.notes: 144/150 on China's Gaokao (national college entrance exam) math paper, reported #1 among models at launch

GPQA Diamond score disclosed for the Doubao-Seed-1.6-thinking variant at the same June 11 2025 Volcano Engine FORCE launch event as the already-tracked Gaokao-math result; corroborated by multiple independent Chinese tech-press recaps (ai.zol.com.cn confirms the June 11 2025 launch date; therouter.ai and other model-pricing pages repeat the identical 81.5 figure).

AIME25 score disclosed for the Doubao-Seed-1.6-thinking variant at the June 11 2025 launch (stated as a 12.3-point gain over the prior Doubao-1.5-thinking model); corroborated across multiple independent Chinese tech-press recaps of the same announcement.
LiveCodeBench v6 score from the ByteDance-authored comparison table.
Table also lists 48.4 for the same checkpoint under an AgentLess 4*10 scaffold; OpenHands figure used as the more standard scaffold.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real: fetched the Sina Finance article (published June 11, 2025, matching releaseDate) directly — it states Doubao's large model scored 144 points and ranked first nationally ('全国第一') on the national Gaokao math paper; the '/150' total is standard knowledge for the Gaokao math paper (not explicitly restated as '150' in the article itself, but a 150-point scale is the well-established format). Fetched ByteDance's own official Seed1.6 techniques blog and confirmed 230B total/23B active MoE params, 256K context window, and the AdaCoT (Adaptive Chain-of-Thought) technique verbatim. Pricing cross-checked: Sina article cites ~0.8 CNY/M input and 2 CNY/M output, which converts to ~$0.11/$0.28 USD — matches the record's price field closely. No changes made to core facts; note field retained as-is since substance is corroborated.

2026-07-16: Filled 4 of the 7 missing core-benchmark gaps for Doubao-Seed-1.6 using ByteDance's own comparison table in the ByteDance-Seed/seed-oss GitHub README (fetched and confirmed via direct curl of the raw markdown, not just a page summarizer) -- added mmlu (90.6), hle (13.9), livecodebench (66.8), and swe-bench (41.8), all credited to the 'Seed1.6-Thinking-0715' column, whose header hyperlinks directly to the Volcano Engine console page for model ID doubao-seed-1-6-thinking, confirming it is the deployed Doubao-Seed-1.6-thinking product (a July-2025 refresh of the same checkpoint line already cited for this file's existing gpqa/aime entries). Left math, humaneval, and mmmu unresolved (omitted_no_evidence): the same GitHub table reports MATH=72.9 and HumanEval=78, but only for a separate 'Seed1.6-Base' column (hyperlinked to the general seed.bytedance.com/en/seed1_6 landing page) -- 'Base' denotes ByteDance's pre-RLHF pretrained checkpoint, a research-only artifact never served as an API product, so I declined to attribute its scores to this file's deployed-model entry given the corpus's known variant-conflation risk (see dataset-audit-2026-07 memory). Plain MMMU was not found anywhere; the Seed1.8 model card (arXiv 2603.20633) cites only MMMU-Pro=61.0 (No-Think mode) for 'Seed-1.6', a distinct, harder benchmark with its own corpus slug (mmmu-pro) that this pass was not asked to fill. Directly checked Epoch AI (epoch.ai/benchmarks) and Vals.ai (vals.ai) -- neither independent tracker has published Doubao-Seed-1.6 coverage. Also checked SuperCLUE (superclueai.com), which tracks Doubao-Seed-1.6-thinking-250715 on its own composite/code/agent scores, but none of those map cleanly onto this corpus's mmlu/hle/math/humaneval/livecodebench/swe-bench/mmmu benchmark definitions, so none of that data was used. No dedicated Seed1.6 arXiv technical report exists (unlike sibling Seed1.5-Thinking/Seed1.5-VL), which is why the official blog's own benchmark tables (embedded as images, not extractable via text fetch) had to be cross-referenced through ByteDance's own GitHub reproduction instead.
